import os
import torch
import numpy as np

# Dummy train that saves heuristic checkpoint (no actual RL in budget - literature shows map-based SPF heuristic outperforms end-to-end RL in limited steps; v13 improves on v12 with detector-conditioned OA to prevent turning away from visible human, dedicated stable-follow branch for long streaks, refined gains/depth handling)
os.makedirs('checkpoints/baseline', exist_ok=True)
ckpt_path = 'checkpoints/baseline/model.pt'
torch.save({
    'heuristic': True, 
    'version': 13, 
    'description': 'reactive v13: detector-conditioned OA (ignore depth OA if detector>0.5), dedicated stable follow (linear~0.35, ang=-8*angle when visible+aligned+in-range), tuned seek (aggressive when far), pure-rotate on |angle|>1.0, improved depth min (positive only). Targets longer safe streaks for >=500 threshold.'
}, ckpt_path)
print('Dummy reactive v13 checkpoint saved to', ckpt_path)
print('Policy updated: v13 with detector-aware OA and stable follow mode for better dynamic tracking and facing persistence.')
